title: cerastium triviale var. glandulosum
intro: Cerastium triviale var. glandulosum, also known as the sticky mouse-ear chickweed, is a perennial herb in the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The health benefits of Cerastium triviale var. glandulosum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or topical cream, making it a versatile remedy for various health concerns.
